commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the cross-OS release-check external-termination test to wait 200 ms before forced child-tree kill and up to 10 s for the spawned child PID to die.

PR surface: Tests 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. deterministic current-main reproduction was run under the read-only review constraints. Source inspection confirms the current test uses a 25 ms forced-kill delay and only waits 2 s for the child to die, matching the reported flaky timeout path.

target.title: fix(ci): increase timeouts in flaky process-group signal test
target.body:
## Problem

The `checks-node-core-tooling` CI job fails intermittently on PR #95400 (and likely others) due to a flaky test:

`test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts` > "forwards external termination to command process groups"

The test fails with `Error: process still alive: <pid>` because the child process doesn't die within the 2s `waitForDead` timeout. This test is unrelated to the PR's changes (which only touch `result-fallback-classifier.ts`).

## Root Cause

On heavily loaded CI runners, the signal cascade (SIGTERM -> wait -> SIGKILL) can take longer than expected:
- `OPENCLAW_CROSS_OS_PROCESS_TREE_KILL_AFTER_MS` was only 25ms before SIGKILL
- `waitForDead` timeout was only 2000ms

## Fix

- Increase `OPENCLAW_CROSS_OS_PROCESS_TREE_KILL_AFTER_MS` from 25ms to 200ms (more reliable signal delivery)
- Increase `waitForDead` timeout from 2000ms to 10000ms (more tolerant of slow CI)

## Verification

This is a test infrastructure fix. The test logic is unchanged -- only the timeouts are relaxed to accommodate loaded CI runners.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
